meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve tutorial hell (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome tutorial hell. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop chasing tutorials and start building. Learn to implement SaaS pricing models using Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver high-value client projects fast.
content.answer_block: Escape tutorial hell by shifting from passive consumption to production-focused engineering. Use Antigravity to scaffold robust architecture and Claude Code to implement dynamic SaaS pricing logic. By focusing on modular, billable features rather than abstract concepts, you transform your workflow into a repeatable, high-margin asset for your freelance clients.
content.intro:
You have spent countless hours watching endless loops of 'ToDo list' tutorials, yet your client dashboard remains empty. The gap between following a video and shipping a production-grade SaaS application is where most freelancers lose their momentum and their sanity. You are stuck in the infinite loop of tutorial hell, consuming knowledge without generating value.

It is time to pivot. Real engineering isn't about memorizing syntax; it's about leveraging high-leverage tools to translate business requirements into revenue-generating features. In this case study, we will dismantle the abstract concept of SaaS pricing and turn it into a concrete, deployable module that will impress your clients and shorten your development cycles significantly.

steps.title: Define the Value Architecture
steps.description: Before writing code, map out your pricing tiers to ensure the data structure supports future scalability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a SaaS pricing module with three tiers: Hobby, Pro, and Enterprise. Include fields for monthly/yearly billing cycles, feature limits, and Stripe API integration hooks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file-path './src/models/pricing.ts' --context 'Use the schema generated by Antigravity to define types and interfaces.'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Billing Logic
steps.description: Use automated scaffolding to set up the foundation of your subscription management service.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a boilerplate service layer for subscription management using TypeScript. Include methods for checkSubscriptionStatus, upgradePlan, and handleStripeWebhook.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement the service layer logic based on the Antigravity output and ensure error handling for failed payments.'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Integration
steps.description: Connect your logic to a real-world payment processor using secure, production-ready patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Stripe integration module that creates a checkout session based on the user's selected pricing tier and returns a redirect URL.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a controller endpoint that utilizes the stripe-integration module to generate checkout sessions securely.'

steps.title: Automate Tier Enforcement
steps.description: Implement middleware to restrict access based on the user's current subscription tier.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a custom middleware function that intercepts incoming requests and verifies if the user has a valid active subscription for the requested feature.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Register the subscription middleware globally and apply it to protected API routes.'

steps.title: Stress Test and Deploy
steps.description: Verify your logic against edge cases to ensure your client's revenue stream is bulletproof.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive test suite for the pricing service covering edge cases like expired credit cards, plan downgrades, and concurrent tier switching.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Execute the generated test suite and fix any identified regressions in the billing logic.'

pro_tips: Always build your pricing models to be 'feature-flag ready' so you can toggle access without redeploying.
Use a database-first approach for pricing to ensure audit logs are captured for every plan transition.
Never hardcode pricing IDs; store them in environment variables or a secure configuration service.
Implement a 'grace period' logic in your middleware to prevent immediate service disruption on failed auto-renewals.
Treat your billing logic as a separate micro-service or module to make it portable across different client projects.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing module is live, integrate a basic analytics dashboard to track conversion rates per tier and iterate based on real user behavior.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop building toy apps and start shipping enterprise-grade SaaS features. Stop waiting for the 'perfect' tutorial and start taking control of your technical output. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
